# UC-22.10.28 · Workstation Security — Unattended Unlocked Sessions in Clinical Pods

## Description

Correlates long gaps between workstation unlock events in nursing stations with active Hyperspace sessions to reduce shoulder-surfing and unauthorized viewing risks.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes epic, windows and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security, epic:audit`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Deploy only on designated clinical OU to limit noise; (2) pair with physical security rounds checklist; (3) educate on clean desk + lock policy. Indexes required: `index=epic`, `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `sourcetype=epic:audit``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security` 4801 (unlock), 4800 (lock), `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit` from same `Workstation_ID`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Deploy only on designated clinical OU to limit noise; (2) pair with physical security rounds checklist; (3) educate on clean desk + lock policy.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN ("4800","4801") earliest=-7d
| eval action=if(EventCode="4800","lock","unlock")
| transaction WorkstationName startswith=eval(action="unlock") endswith=eval(action="lock") maxspan=8h
| eval idle_during=if(duration>3600 AND eventcount<3,1,0)
| where idle_during=1
| table WorkstationName, duration, user, _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Workstation Security — Unattended Unlocked Sessions in Clinical Pods (§164.310(b))** — Correlates long gaps between workstation unlock events in nursing stations with active Hyperspace sessions to reduce shoulder-surfing and unauthorized viewing risks.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` 4801 (unlock), 4800 (lock), `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit` from same `Workstation_ID`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=windows, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **action** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Groups related events into transactions — prefer `maxspan`/`maxpause`/`maxevents` for bounded memory.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **idle_during** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where idle_during=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, WorkstationName, action, duration, idle_during, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Histogram (session durations), Table (worst workstations).

## SPL

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN ("4800","4801") earliest=-7d
| eval action=if(EventCode="4800","lock","unlock")
| transaction WorkstationName startswith=eval(action="unlock") endswith=eval(action="lock") maxspan=8h
| eval idle_during=if(duration>3600 AND eventcount<3,1,0)
| where idle_during=1
| table WorkstationName, duration, user, _time
```

## Visualization

Histogram (session durations), Table (worst workstations).
